channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Dado Banatao: Filipino Innovator Behind Modern Computing and AI Foundations
item.description: Dado Banatao, a Filipino engineer from Iguig, Cagayan, played a pivotal role in shaping the hardware foundations that made today’s artificial intelligence possible. He passed away on December 25, 2025...

item.title: iFixit Teardown Gives Apple's New MacBook Neo a 6/10 Repairability Score, Best in 14 Years
item.description: Apple's recently launched MacBook Neo, positioned as the company's most affordable laptop starting at $599, has received praise from repair experts at iFixit for significant improvements in repairabil...

item.title: Steven Dubois Defends 500m World Short Track Title in Montreal
item.description: Steven Dubois of Terrebonne, Quebec, captured gold in the men's 500-metre at the ISU Short Track World Championships held in Montreal, successfully defending the title he won at last month's Milano-Co...

item.title: US General Pledges Transparent Investigation into Iranian School Missile Strike
item.description: A US investigation into the Tomahawk missile strike on a girls' school in Minab, southern Iran, which resulted in at least 168 deaths, primarily schoolgirls, is currently underway. General David Petra...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Pushes Freight Costs Up, Pakistan Trade Faces Challenges
item.description: Karachi-based industry experts and traders are expressing concern over the impact of the US-Israel war on Iran on Pakistan’s trade and supply chains. While pharmaceutical and textile sectors report no...

item.title: German Philosopher Jürgen Habermas, Key Figure in Post-War Discourse, Passes Away
item.description: Jürgen Habermas, a towering figure in German philosophy and a profound influence on the nation’s post-war identity, has passed away at the age of 96. Known for his groundbreaking theory of political c...

item.title: Kelly McGillis: From Hollywood Stardom to a Life of Recovery and Advocacy
item.description: Kelly McGillis rose to fame in the 1980s as a leading Hollywood actress, best known for her roles in Top Gun, Witness, and The Accused. She was celebrated for her talent and on-screen chemistry with s...

item.title: Eastern Cape ANC councillor under scrutiny for extended rent-free chalet stay
item.description: A ward councillor from the Eastern Cape, representing the ANC, is facing scrutiny after reports emerged that he has allegedly stayed rent-free for three years in a beachfront chalet at the Gonubie Car...

item.title: EKEDC Schedules Three-Day Power Interruption in Lekki for Network Upgrade
item.description: Eko Electricity Distribution Company (EKEDC) don announce say dem go cut light for Lekki area for three days, from March 13 to 15, 2026, between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. each day. According to the notice wey...

item.title: Briatore Confirms Alpine F1 Stake Talks with Mercedes, Not Toto Wolff
item.description: Flavio Briatore, acting as the de facto boss of Alpine F1, confirmed that Mercedes is in discussions to acquire a 24% stake in the Renault-owned team. Briatore stressed that the talks are with Mercede...

item.title: Reflections on the Challenges and Responsibilities of Serving as an MCA
item.description: In this article, Governor Irungu Kang'ata shares insights from his political journey, focusing on his early experiences as a councillor, now called Member of County Assembly (MCA). He recalls being su...

item.title: FCT Enrols 480 Journalists in Health Insurance Scheme, Plans to Cover 50 More
item.description: For the first time, the Federal Capital Territory Health Services and Environment Secretariat don launch free health insurance programme for journalists for FCT under the Federal Capital Territory Hea...

item.title: Steven Spielberg Defends Ballet and Opera Amid Timothee Chalamet's Comments
item.description: Steven Spielberg has responded to Timothee Chalamet's recent controversial comments suggesting that ballet and opera are losing relevance. Speaking at the SXSW festival in Texas, Spielberg highlighted...

item.title: Veteran Actress Madhu Malhotra Passes Away at 71
item.description: Madhu Malhotra, a familiar face in Hindi cinema during the 1970s and 1980s, passed away in Mumbai on Friday at the age of 71, as reported by 'Film Information'. She gained recognition for her roles in...

item.title: Taber, Alberta receives funding for arena reconstruction and community fundraising efforts
item.description: Taber, a southern Alberta town east of Lethbridge, has been awarded $50,000 as one of the provincial winners of the Kraft Hockeyville contest. This comes after an explosion in December last year rende...

item.title: Rising Oil Prices Could Weigh Heavily on Pakistan’s Economy and Current Account
item.description: Karachi is facing a potential economic storm as oil prices soar above $100 due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. Former finance minister Hafiz Pasha warns that Pakistan’s GDP could shrink by 1.0 to...

item.title: Discrepancy Between Oil Futures and Physical Oil Prices Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the current divergence between oil futures traded on the market and the prices of actual physical crude oil. While futures, sometimes called 'paper oil,' reflect expectations tha...

item.title: Live Updates on U.S. and Israeli Military Operations Against Iran
item.description: The live coverage tracks the escalating conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran that began on February 28, 2026, with surprise aerial bombardments on multiple Iranian cities amid ongoing ...

item.title: Study Finds Prostate Cancer Screening Comparable to Breast Cancer Checks
item.description: A recent study indicates that screening for prostate cancer could be as effective as routine mammograms for breast cancer. The research, conducted at the German Cancer Research Centre and presented at...

item.title: Court Allows Al-Quds Rally in Toronto; Police Make Two Arrests During Event
item.description: On Saturday, downtown Toronto saw a heavy police presence during the Al-Quds rally after an Ontario judge denied the provincial government's request for an injunction to stop the event. The court cite...
